author: PC Gamer
text:
Alongside its new expansion, Warhammer 40K: Rogue Trader got a massive patch buffing its weakest classes and companion
Uralon the Cool.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Nintendo's legal battle with Palworld looks shakier than ever: It may only get '$30,000 chump change' if it wins at all
It looks like Pocketpair will come out of this largely unfazed.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Alright, who's the joker trying to charge $100 on Steam for an almost entirely AI-generated game?
C'mon man.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Make some noise': Vanillaware reportedly wants more of its games on PC, but it's up to the publishers
The studio behind Odin Sphere and Unicorn Overlord "totally wants" more of its games on PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
A little known rendering technique that can create low-cost, photo-real graphics may be about to have its big moment in game development
Sheer art attack.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New Crimson Desert patch lets you hoard 100 pets and gives you more chainsaw access
More pets, more chainsaws. What else are videogames for?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Sim racing is flourishing, but what happened to the arcade racer?
SimRacing Expo 2026 showed how much the genre has grown, while the casual racing scene is now largely concentrated in one series.

author: PC Gamer
text:
I wish Bungie would stop designing excellent multiplayer shooters and go back to making fantasy strategy games where you blow up zombies with dwarf bombs
Extraction shooters are great and all, but I'd give my right leg for more Myth.

author: PC Gamer
text:
It's bittersweet that the best time to play Destiny 2 in years coincides with its end, but I'm happy for the new players who get to experience its most liberating iteration yet
This is one of the best quality of life patches we've had in a long time.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This massively ambitious The Witcher 3 mod aims to recreate its biggest cut questline, and you could be the voice of Geralt of Rivia
The War Project will add 12 new quests across 10 more hours of monster hunting.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Palworld 1.0 won't wipe your data, 'but you should' says Pocketpair's publishing boss
"We believe that starting a new character will give you the best experience of Palworld".

author: PC Gamer
text:
Dying Light 2 is a platform now, as the Breach update opens up Techland's zombie survival game 'even more to UGC content'
"Patch 1.28 is something we wanted to do for a long time."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Don't expect the Death Stranding movie to retell the story of the games, as its director says the film will be set in his 'own corner of the world'
But you "might see some characters from the game pop up".

author: PC Gamer
text:
Everything we know about Silent Hill: Townfall and its foggy Scottish town
Townfall's got medical trauma, fog, and a radio. Sounds like Silent Hill to me.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Microsoft is looking to speed up development of future Elder Scrolls, Fallout, and Halo games
A new report suggests Microsoft may also be giving thought to spinning off its Xbox division or selling it entirely.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Tracing blueprints in Paralives is so easy it freed me from 25 years of The Sims' grid-based tyranny
One tiny mod for Paralives has finally made my childhood dreams of more realistic life sims floor plans possible.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Even a fantasy game like Skyrim won't let me be a trillionaire
I can be a werewolf or a vampire, sure, but nothing as far-fetched as a trillionaire.

author: PC Gamer
text:
RPG Maker forums are closing, nearly 15 years of knowledge and online culture are at risk of being wiped
Gotcha Gotcha Games is launching a new forum, which is good, but says it won't archive the old one, which is very bad.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 11 is one of the greatest MMOs of our time
Revisiting an enduring MMORPG.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Over the Hill's Next Fest demo promises a stylized off-roading game for people who actually like to drive
Forza Horizon is fine, I guess, but for my money this is where it's at.

author: PC Gamer
text:
ProtoArc EM25 ergonomic mouse review
Rough around the edges.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Consumer rights champion and tech-whizz Louis Rossman is taking Samsung to court over a failed 990 Pro SSD it says it can't replace, even though Amazon has plenty of them in stock
The fact that they now cost $949 instead of $300 surely has nothing to do with it, right?
